mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: ImportError with openai 1.100
mission.description: [Bug]: ImportError with openai 1.100. This issue affects 22+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### What happened?

About an hour ago, [openai 1.100](https://pypi.org/project/openai/1.100.0/) was released. This causes an ImportError in LiteLLM's OpenAI integration.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: ImportError with openai 1.100
steps.description:
## Update OpenAI dependency to version 1.100.0 and refactor response configuration types for consistency

## Relevant issues

## Pre-Submission checklist

**Please complete all items before asking a LiteLLM maintainer to review your PR**

## Type

🚄 Infrastructure

## Changes

```yaml
  File ".../litellm/litellm_core_utils/core_helpers.py", line 8, in <module>
    from litellm.types.llms.openai import AllMessageValues
  File ".../litellm/types/llms/openai.py", line 36, in <module>
    from openai.types.responses.response import (
ImportError: cannot import name 'ResponseTextConfig' from 'openai.t
```

steps.title: Upgrade litellm to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade litellm:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --namespace litellm
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm
helm list -n litellm
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: ImportError with openai 1.100 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
